behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: three collectors + aggregation payload into the summary step. A unit test of only the aggregator risks missing wiring/contract mismatches between source adapters and assembler (MOCK-MISS). Rejected System because behavior text is about "content passed" and does not require real network/OS/subprocess; in-process source fakes can reproduce missing-source wiring faults (REAL-INFRA no). Rejected Agentic because correctness here is payload completeness before generation, not model output quality (LLM-DEP no).
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collection/assembly modules together with deterministic fake responses from each source client, execute one report build, and assert the summary-input payload contains sections/fields from metrics endpoint, issue tracker API, and deployment log.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is graceful-degradation orchestration across modules: source adapter error -> report assembler fallback note -> continued report generation. Unit is too low because failure appears at error propagation boundary (MOCK-MISS). Rejected System: behavior does not require real network outage mechanics; an in-process client that raises timeout/unavailable catches fallback-path bugs. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: assertion is fallback control flow and note insertion, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run report generation with real orchestration modules, configure one source client stub to raise an unavailable error, and assert output report exists, contains `data unavailable` in that section, and still includes other sections.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on what the summary generator actually writes from input values; this is model-output fidelity, not just plumbing. Deterministic stubs would always pass and hide hallucination/omission/miscalibration failures (LLM-DEP yes, deterministic-assertion trap). Rejected Workflow: behavior names a single generation judgment step, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence. Rejected Integration: would only validate prompt wiring/format, not semantic accuracy of generated narrative.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real summary-generation model against representative inputs with known key values, score outputs for factual inclusion/correctness of those values (with clear acceptance rubric and repeated runs for variance), and fail on omissions or numeric distortions beyond threshold.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is end-to-end infrastructure: external APIs + outbound email transport + inbox delivery/formatting. In-process mocks can hide SMTP/API auth, TLS, serialization, or transport issues (REAL-INFRA yes, MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Agentic: behavior text focuses on delivery to inbox "correctly" (pipeline/infrastructure success), not evaluating summary semantic quality. Rejected Workflow: not a multi-agent decision pipeline requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Run the scheduled script in an environment using real test endpoints (or production-like staging) and a real mailbox, then verify recipient inbox receives the report email with expected subject/body/attachments and all sections present.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans infrastructure boundaries: delivery-network failure plus durable local persistence across runs. Real filesystem/process-run boundaries and real unreachable delivery conditions are where hidden failures occur (path/permissions/serialization/reload/retry timing). Integration fakes can miss cross-run durability and OS-level file behavior (REAL-INFRA yes). Rejected Workflow: no multiple agentic decisions; this is reliability orchestration. Rejected Agentic: independent of model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute run #1 with email endpoint intentionally unreachable, assert report file is written to disk in retry queue; execute run #2 in a fresh process with email restored, assert queued report is sent and queue artifact is cleared/marked delivered.
